name: Charlie Kirk
summary: Charlie Kirk's character and pattern of behavior are marked by a consistent promotion of misinformation, conspiracy theories, and divisive rhetoric. He has repeatedly spread false claims about COVID-19, voter fraud, and social issues, and has advocated for extreme positions on topics such as abortion, gun control, and LGBTQ rights. Kirk has also demonstrated a pattern of racist and xenophobic behavior, making derogatory comments about people of color, Muslims, and other minority groups, and promoting the "Great Replacement" conspiracy theory. His actions and statements have been criticized for threatening academic freedom, promoting hate speech, and eroding trust in institutions. Overall, Kirk's behavior suggests a disregard for facts, a willingness to exploit divisive issues for personal gain, and a commitment to promoting a radical, extremist ideology.

entries.fact: Charlie Kirk volunteered for the successful U.S. Senate campaign of Illinois Republican Mark Kirk.
Charlie Kirk co-founded the conservative student organization Turning Point USA (TPUSA), which later expanded into affiliate groups such as Turning Point Action and Turning Point Faith.
Charlie Kirk alleged that he had lost a slot to attend West Point to a candidate of 'a different ethnicity and gender'.
Charlie Kirk promoted the Cultural Marxism conspiracy theory and called universities 'islands of totalitarianism'.
Charlie Kirk said in an interview with Wired during the 2016 Republican National Convention that he would vote for Donald Trump, despite not being a big fan of his.
Charlie Kirk's organization Turning Point USA made 'misleading financial claims' and its leaders enriched themselves while advocating for Trump, according to a ProPublica investigation.
Charlie Kirk's salary from Turning Point USA increased from $27,000 to nearly $300,000, and he bought an $855,000 condo in Longboat Key, Florida.
Charlie Kirk promoted false allegations of electoral fraud in the 2020 election.
The Falkirk Center for Faith and Liberty, co-founded by Charlie Kirk and Jerry Falwell Jr., spent at least $50,000 on Facebook advertisements promoting Trump and Republican candidates.

Charlie Kirk released his book The MAGA Doctrine, a manifesto for the Make America Great Again movement, in which he wrote that the Republican Party is 'in some sense no longer a conservative party, no longer the party of Reagan, but instead a Trump-remade populist party'.

Charlie Kirk was described by The New York Times as exemplifying 'walking the line between mainstream conservative opinion and outright disinformation' and that 'with a powerful ally in the president's eldest son, Donald Trump Jr., Mr. Kirk both amplifies the president's message and helps shape it'.

Charlie Kirk said at a Council for National Policy meeting that Democrats shutting down college campuses would remove opportunities for ballot harvesting and voter fraud, and that it would be beneficial to keep campuses closed.
Turning Point USA's Professor Watchlist and School Board Watchlist have been criticized for threatening academic freedom and leading to the targeted harassment of academics.
Turning Point USA's Professor Watchlist was briefly suspended by its web host in 2019 due to criticism of its activities.

Charlie Kirk wrote on Twitter that Turning Point Action and Students for Trump were sending more than 80 'buses of patriots to D.C. to fight for this president' ahead of the January 6 U.S. Capitol attack.
Charlie Kirk's organizations sent seven buses with 350 students to the January 6 event in Washington, D.C., which led to the storming of the U.S. Capitol.
Charlie Kirk received $1.25 million from Publix heiress Julie Fancelli to fund the buses to the January 6 event.
Charlie Kirk paid $60,000 for Kimberly Guilfoyle to speak at the rally preceding the January 6 U.S. Capitol attack.
Charlie Kirk called for a 'patriot' to bail out of jail the man who broke into Nancy Pelosi's house and attacked and tried to murder her husband with a hammer.
Charlie Kirk pleaded the Fifth Amendment privilege against self-incrimination when appearing before the U.S. House Select Committee on the January 6 Attack.
Charlie Kirk called for the imprisonment or the death penalty of Joe Biden for 'crimes against America'.

A Brookings Institution study found Charlie Kirk's podcast contained the second-highest proportion of false, misleading, and unsubstantiated statements among 36,603 episodes produced by 79 prominent political podcasters.
Charlie Kirk recorded a statement on what the Bible says about money.
Charlie Kirk advocated for a range of conservative positions, including opposition to abortion, gun control, DEI programs, and LGBTQ rights.
Charlie Kirk promoted COVID-19 misinformation and the white genocide conspiracy theory.
Charlie Kirk was appointed by Trump to the U.S. Air Force Academy Board of Visitors.
Charlie Kirk stated that it should be legal to burn a rainbow or Black Lives Matter flag in public.
Charlie Kirk was fatally shot by a rooftop sniper while speaking at a TPUSA debate event at Utah Valley University.
The New York Times said Charlie Kirk symbolized hope for the Christian right.
Kirk's rhetoric was described as divisive, racist, xenophobic, and extreme by groups that studied hate speech, including the Southern Poverty Law Center.
Charlie Kirk told a college audience that the concept of white privilege is a myth and a 'racist idea'.
Charlie Kirk tweeted a false claim about human trafficking arrests, which originated from the conspiracy site 8chan, and later deleted it without explanation.
Charlie Kirk falsely claimed that protesters in the French yellow vests movement chanted 'We want Trump'.

Charlie Kirk claimed he never said that a less-qualified candidate of a different gender and persuasion was accepted to the U.S. Military Academy instead of him, despite previously making the statement.
Charlie Kirk said, 'I believe marriage is one man, one woman', but added that gay people should be allowed in the conservative movement.
Charlie Kirk spread falsehoods about voter fraud ahead of the 2020 U.S. presidential election.
Charlie Kirk was considered a 'big name' social influencer in Rudy Giuliani's communications plan to overturn the 2020 election.
Charlie Kirk spread false information and conspiracy theories about COVID-19 on social media, including claiming that hydroxychloroquine was '100% effective in treating the virus'.
Charlie Kirk falsely stated that during the 2009 swine flu pandemic it 'took President Barack Obama millions infected and over 1,000 deaths' to declare a public health emergency.

Charlie Kirk described the public health measure of social distancing prohibitions in churches as a Democratic plot against Christianity and made the unfounded assertion that authorities in Wuhan, China, were burning patients.
Charlie Kirk said he refused to abide by mask requirements because 'the science around masks is very questionable'.
Charlie Kirk led a Stop the Steal protest at the Maricopa Tabulation Center in Phoenix, promoting false and disproven claims of fraud in the election.

Charlie Kirk said that Democratic immigration policies were aimed at 'diminishing and decreasing white demographics in America' and called for Texas to 'deputize a citizen force and put them on the border' to protect 'white demographics in America'.
Charlie Kirk promoted misleading claims about the efficacy and safety of COVID-19 vaccines.
Charlie Kirk called mandatory requirements for students to take the COVID-19 vaccine 'medical apartheid'.
Charlie Kirk said 'the facts are that there are only two genders; that transgenderism and gender 'fluidity' are lies that hurt people and abuse kids' in an op-ed for The American Mind.
Charlie Kirk called the separation of church and state in the United States a 'fabrication'.
Charlie Kirk routinely made anti-LGBTQ remarks, opposing transgender rights and medical care, and asserted that there is an 'LGBTQ agenda'.
Charlie Kirk claimed that being gay was an 'error' and likened homosexuality to drug addiction.

Charlie Kirk criticized the Supreme Court's decision in Obergefell v. Hodges, calling LGBTQ activists the 'alphabet mafia' and claiming that the movement is not 'just about two dudes being able to get married'.

Charlie Kirk said that 'all men are created equal in the eyes of God, all men and women, but not all cultures are created equal. To say that, you get attacked in every direction, but excuse me when I say that Western civilization is the best that humanity has produced. It's an outgrowth of the Bible.'

Charlie Kirk said, 'I think it's worth it, I think it's worth to have a cost of, unfortunately, some gun deaths every single year so that we can have the Second Amendment to protect our other God-given rights.'

Charlie Kirk said, 'One of the reasons we're living through a constitutional crisis is that we no longer have a Christian nation, but we have a Christian form of government, and they're incompatible. You cannot have liberty if you do not have a Christian population.'
Charlie Kirk said, 'This is a Christian state. I'd like to see it stay that way.'
Charlie Kirk argued that abortion is murder and should be illegal, and opposed exceptions for rape, including for children as young as 10.
Charlie Kirk compared abortion to the Holocaust, and said that abortion is worse.
Charlie Kirk promoted debunked claims about George Floyd, including that he was 'illegally counterfeiting currency' and had once 'put a gun to a pregnant woman's stomach'.
Charlie Kirk repeatedly promoted the false claim that the medical examiner who performed George Floyd's autopsy declared Floyd had died of an overdose.

Charlie Kirk called for the elimination of Jasmine Crockett's congressional district, justifying the erasure of her district by claiming she was a part of an 'attempt to eliminate the white population in this country'.
Charlie Kirk opposed the Paris Agreement on climate change.
Charlie Kirk asserted that the increasing presence of African Americans in American politics resulted in the Black community being 'worse off'.
Charlie Kirk began the 'Exposing Critical Racism Tour' of a number of campuses and off-campus venues to 'fight racist theories on America's college campuses!'.
Charlie Kirk told young women to go to college for the purpose of finding husbands and 'embrace their roles as mothers and homemakers'.
Charlie Kirk said that transgender women in women's locker rooms should be 'taken care of the way we used to take care of things in the 1950s and '60s'.

Charlie Kirk said, 'One issue I think that is so against our senses, so against the natural law and, dare I say, a throbbing middle finger to God, is the transgender thing happening in America right now'.
Charlie Kirk said that 'prowling Blacks go around for fun to go target white people' in urban America.

Charlie Kirk reacted to Representative Sheila Jackson Lee's statement on affirmative action by saying, 'If we would have said three weeks ago [...] that Joy Reid and Michelle Obama and Sheila Jackson Lee and Ketanji Brown Jackson were affirmative-action picks, we would have been called racist. But now they're comin' out and they're saying it for us!'

Charlie Kirk said on The Charlie Kirk Show that 'Jewish donors have been the number 1 funding mechanism of radical, open border, neoliberal, quasi-Marxist policies ... This is a beast created by secular Jews, and now it's coming for Jews', and also suggested that these Jews control 'not just the colleges; it's the nonprofits, it's the movies, it's Hollywood, it's all of it.'
Charlie Kirk called on American Jews to stop 'subsidizing your own demise by supporting institutions that breed Anti-Semites and endorse genocidal killers.'
Charlie Kirk described Martin Luther King Jr. as 'awful ... not a good person' and as someone who is admired only because he said 'one thing he didn't actually believe'.
Charlie Kirk condemned the Civil Rights Act of 1964, calling its passage a 'huge mistake' and alleging that it had created a 'permanent DEI-type bureaucracy'.
Charlie Kirk said, 'The great replacement strategy, which is well under way every single day in our southern border, is a strategy to replace white rural America with something different'.
Charlie Kirk said, 'The American Democrat party hates this country. They wanna see it collapse. They love it when America becomes less white.'

Charlie Kirk posted 'The ' Great Replacement ' is not a theory, it's a reality', alongside a Fox News headline that falsely claimed: '7.2M illegals entered the U.S. under Biden administration, an amount greater than population of 36 states.'

Charlie Kirk said that a 'myth' had been created around Martin Luther King Jr. which had 'grown totally out of control' and that King was currently 'the most honored, worshiped, even deified person of the 20th century' despite 'most people' supposedly disliking him during his life.
Charlie Kirk called Martin Luther King Jr. 'just a man ... a very flawed one at that' and a 'mythological anti-racist creation of the 1960s'.
Charlie Kirk said 'If I see a Black pilot, I'm going to be like, 'Boy, I hope he's qualified.'
Charlie Kirk blamed DEI programs for national aviation issues.
Charlie Kirk called Ketanji Brown Jackson a 'recipient of affirmative action' and said she was nominated for the Supreme Court because of her race.
Charlie Kirk called for Trump to propose a nationwide ban of gender-affirming care for transgender people.
Charlie Kirk called for the imprisonment of doctors who perform gender-affirming care and demanded 'Nuremberg-style' trials for them.

Charlie Kirk criticized YouTuber Ms. Rachel for a post that celebrated Pride Month, quoting the Bible verse 'love thy neighbor' and arguing that she was being selective, and told her that 'thou shall lay with another man shall be stoned to death' according to Leviticus 18.

Charlie Kirk stated that 'America does not need more visas for people from India', arguing that the American workforce has become dominated by Indian-American immigrants, effectively decreasing job opportunities for Americans.
Charlie Kirk commented that America would still be America if it were ethnically 90% Indian, as long as they were Christian Indians.

Charlie Kirk elaborated on Hinduism and his disapproval of its morality due to its polytheism, stating: 'When you have multiple gods, you get different moralities. And the West has largely embraced the idea that there is a standard of conduct, or a best way to live.'

Charlie Kirk responded to an inquiry about his claim not being inclusive of other religious worldviews, saying: 'I don't seek to be inclusive, I seek what is best. And the Ten Commandments are what is best. Would it be offensive to a young Hindu kid? Maybe, maybe not. But it also is a reminder they're living in a country that's a monotheistic country.'
Charlie Kirk defended Elon Musk from charges of antisemitism by claiming the post's charge against Jewish communities was accurate.
Charlie Kirk blamed the high death toll of the July 2025 Central Texas floods on DEI.

Charlie Kirk accused Democrats of spreading a 'false narrative' that 'there is a relentless assault against Black people on behalf of white people', and said 'White individuals are actually more likely to be attacked, especially even per capita, by Black individuals in this country.'
Charlie Kirk admitted that Turning Point USA had accepted funding from the fossil fuel industry and spoke out against targeting fossil fuels.
Charlie Kirk was a speaker for the annual conference held by the anti-Muslim advocacy group ACT for America in Washington DC

Charlie Kirk wrote the book Campus Battlefield: How Conservatives Can WIN the Battle on Campus and Why It Matters, which was described as a 'hot mess' and 'nothing more than a marketing pitch for TPUSA' by Adam Rubenstein in The Weekly Standard
Charlie Kirk and Candace Owens claimed in a video that there is no factual data to back up global warming, which was rated inaccurate by Science Feedback.
Charlie Kirk warned that climate activism would erode American sovereignty and private property, describing it as a Trojan Horse for Marxism and likening it to pseudo-paganism.
Charlie Kirk said that the southern border matters more than the Ukrainian border and called for Republican leaders to label the situation at the southern border an invasion.
Charlie Kirk argued that 'It's not Islamophobia to notice that Muslims want to import values into the West that seek to destabilize our civilization.'
Charlie Kirk said of Palestine, 'I don't think the place exists.'
Charlie Kirk called for the use of force against migrants at the U.S.–Mexico border, including the use of tear gas, rubber bullets, and whips.
Charlie Kirk blamed the Chinese government and drug cartels for the opioid crisis in the United States.
Charlie Kirk said that immigration to the United States should be completely stopped.

Charlie Kirk advanced pro-Russian talking points about the Russo-Ukrainian War, characterizing the tensions as a 'border dispute' and repeating false claims from Russian state media that Ukrainian forces had been shelling a Russian separatist enclave.
Charlie Kirk opposed the U.S. sending arms to Ukraine or providing financial support to the country.
Charlie Kirk opposed U.S. involvement in the Iran–Israel War, warning that a prolonged war would destabilize the region and could trigger a refugee crisis and civil war in Iran.
Charlie Kirk called Mehdi Hasan a 'neurotic lunatic' and said, 'Send him back to the country he came from. Holy cow! Get him off TV. Revoke his visa.'
Charlie Kirk called U.S. representative Ilhan Omar a 'terrorist sympathizer' and called for her deportation.

Charlie Kirk promoted a conspiracy theory alleging the Israeli government knew that Hamas was going to launch the October 7 attacks on Israel in 2023, and that Benjamin Netanyahu allowed it to go ahead as part of a plan to remain in power.
Charlie Kirk promoted the false claim that Haitians in Springfield, Ohio, were eating residents' pets and other wildlife.

Charlie Kirk offered an 'apology' to the Russian people, stating 'very few Americans want war with you' and that 'the people obsessed with fighting you forever' were a minority 'on their way out of power'.
Charlie Kirk said that no non-Jewish person his age has a longer or clearer record of support for Israel, sympathy with the Jewish people, or opposition to antisemitism than he does
Charlie Kirk wrote on Twitter that Islam is the sword the left is using to slit the throat of America

Charlie Kirk posted that 24 years ago a group of Muslims killed 2,753 people on 9/11, now a Muslim Socialist is on pace to run New York City, which was described as a gross and Islamophobic post by liberal Fox News commentator Jessica Tarlov
Charlie Kirk called Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskyy a 'CIA puppet' and 'gangster' who 'sent his own people to a senseless massacre'.
Charlie Kirk stated that taking Taiwan would probably start a nuclear war and that the US should have taken Taiwan in 1950.

Charlie Kirk opposed a bipartisan bill to expand anti-BDS laws, which punish the boycott of Israel, saying it would 'only create more antisemitism, and play into growing narratives that Israel is running the U.S. government'.
Charlie Kirk claimed that the philosophical foundation of anti-whiteness has been largely financed by Jewish donors
Charlie Kirk warned his followers against hatred of Jews, calling it evil and demonic

Charlie Kirk texted associates that Jewish donors play into all the stereotypes and he felt pushed to leave the pro-Israel cause as donors tried to bully him by withholding funding for associating with Candace Owens and Tucker Carlson
Charlie Kirk disagreed with Trump's decision to send more military aid to Ukraine, saying: 'We were against it with Biden. Why would we be for it now? Unless it gets us to a peace settlement'.

Trump administration officials and congressional Republicans immediately blamed Democrats and liberal beliefs for the shooting without evidence, drawing allegations of exploiting the death for political gain.

Charlie Kirk wrote the book The MAGA Doctrine: The Only Ideas That Will Win the Future, which was criticized for its content, including Kirk's musings about Donald Trump and his book 'The Art of The Deal' potentially being considered a 'religious tract'
Charlie Kirk's Twitter account was locked for spreading misinformation about mail-in votes.
Charlie Kirk falsely claimed there is no evidence of global warming and scientists don't know the cause in a viral Turning Point USA video.
Charlie Kirk likened concern about climate change to 'pseudo-paganism'.
Charlie Kirk called Ketanji Brown Jackson a 'recipient of affirmative action' who is 'unqualified' for the Supreme Court
Charlie Kirk defended Elon Musk's antisemitism, stating 'Some of the largest financiers of left-wing anti-white causes have been Jewish Americans'

Charlie Kirk wrote the book Right Wing Revolution: How to Beat the Woke and Save the West, in which he argues that the United States is 'under threat from a lethal ideology that seeks to humiliate and erase anyone that does not bow at its altar'
Charlie Kirk flipped on Martin Luther King Jr., attacking the growing 'myth' of his legacy
Charlie Kirk stated that the death toll from Texas flooding would not have been as high if it wasn't for Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) initiatives
Charlie Kirk accused Rep. Jasmine Crockett of being part of an 'attempt to eliminate the white population in this country'
Far-right activists such as Laura Loomer called for violence and retaliation in the aftermath of Kirk's assassination.
Charlie Kirk once said prominent Black women didn't have 'brain processing power' to be taken seriously
Charlie Kirk once said Jewish money was ruining US culture
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth instructed his staff to identify and discipline service members who either mocked or expressed approval of Kirk's murder.

More than 100 people, including lawyers, doctors, first responders, and over 50 high school teachers and college professors, had been censured, suspended, dismissed, or were under investigation for expressing criticism of Kirk or allegedly celebrating his death.
Charlie Kirk floated a conspiracy theory that Netanyahu issued a “stand down” order as a means to bolster his own political standing after the October 7th attacks.
